FHA vs VA Loan

FHA vs. VA loans

VA loans offer 0% down and no monthly mortgage insurance for those who qualify — here's how they compare to FHA, and how to choose.

Compare

FHA vs. VA loans

If you're an eligible veteran, active-duty service member, or surviving spouse, a VA loan is usually the stronger option — but FHA is open to everyone. Here's the comparison.

FeatureFHA LoanVA Loan
Down payment3.5%0% down
EligibilityOpen to most buyersVeterans, service members, eligible spouses
Mortgage insuranceMIP requiredNo monthly MI (one-time funding fee)
Credit flexibility580+ typicalLender-set; often flexible
Funding/upfront fee1.75% upfront MIPVA funding fee (waived for some)

Which should you choose?

If you qualify for a VA loan, it's hard to beat: no down payment and no monthly mortgage insurance. FHA is the go-to when you're not VA-eligible but still want a low down payment and flexible credit. We can confirm your VA eligibility and compare both.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

Is a VA loan better than an FHA loan?+
For eligible veterans and service members, VA loans are usually better — no down payment and no monthly mortgage insurance. FHA is the strong alternative if you're not VA-eligible.
Can I use an FHA loan if I'm a veteran?+
Yes, veterans can use FHA, but most who are VA-eligible choose VA for the 0% down and no monthly MI. We can compare both for your situation.
Does a VA loan have mortgage insurance?+
No monthly mortgage insurance, but there's a one-time VA funding fee (which some veterans are exempt from). FHA charges both upfront and annual MIP.
Which is easier to qualify for, FHA or VA?+
Both are flexible. VA has no set minimum credit score (lenders set their own), while FHA formally allows 580 for 3.5% down.
